Data Types.details: A standard 32-bit signed integer type used for whole numbers.
A larger integer type for numbers that exceed the capacity of a standard 32-bit int.
An unsigned integer type typically used for sizes of objects and array indexing.
A 32-bit floating point type for real numbers with approximately 7 decimal digits of precision.
A 64-bit floating point type for real numbers with approximately 15 decimal digits of precision.
Represents a single character, usually stored as an 8-bit integer mapping to ASCII.
A type that can hold one of two values: true or false.
A dynamic-size sequence of characters provided by the C++ Standard Library.
Instructs the compiler to automatically deduce the type of a variable from its initializer.

Keywords.details: Indicates that the value of an expression can be evaluated at compile-time.
A typesafe literal representing a null pointer, replacing the old NULL macro.
Specifies that a variable's value cannot be modified after initialization.
A hint to the compiler to replace the function call with the function's body to reduce overhead.
Keywords.example: inline int square(int x) { return x * x; }

Keywords.details: When applied to local variables, they persist across function calls; when applied to globals, they have internal linkage.
Declares a variable or function that is defined in another source file.
Allows a member function to be overridden in a derived class for polymorphism.
Explicitly marks a member function as overriding a virtual function from a base class.
Prevents a virtual function from being overridden or a class from being inherited.

Control Flow.details: Executes a block of code if a specified condition evaluates to true.
Selects one of many code blocks to be executed based on the value of an expression.
Repeatedly executes a block of code as long as a condition is met, typically with an iterator.
Iterates over every element in a container or range conveniently.
Executes a block of code repeatedly as long as the condition is true.
Guarantees that the code block is executed at least once before checking the condition.

Control Flow.desc: Exit the current loop or switch
Control Flow.details: Immediately terminates the execution of the innermost loop or switch statement.

Control Flow.desc: Skip the rest of the current loop iteration
Control Flow.details: Skips the remaining code in the current loop iteration and moves to the next one.

Pointers & Memory.details: Accesses the value stored at the memory address held by the pointer.
Returns the memory address of the specified variable.
Allocates memory for a single object of the specified type on the heap.
Releases the memory previously allocated by 'new' for a single object.
Allocates memory for an array of objects on the heap.
Releases the memory previously allocated by 'new[]' for an array.
A smart pointer that ensures only one pointer owns the resource at a time.
A smart pointer that allows multiple pointers to share ownership of the same resource.
A smart pointer that holds a non-owning reference to an object managed by shared_ptr.
The preferred way to create a unique_ptr to avoid potential memory leaks during allocation.
The preferred way to create a shared_ptr, often more efficient than direct 'new'.

STL Containers.details: A resizable array container that provides random access and efficient tail insertions.
A container for a fixed-size array, safer and more feature-rich than C-style arrays.
An indexed sequence container that allows fast insertion and deletion at both ends.
A container that supports constant time insertion and removal from anywhere in the container.
A collection of key-value pairs, sorted by keys, typically implemented as a balanced tree.
A collection of key-value pairs, organized into buckets using a hash function for fast access.
A container that stores unique elements in a specific sorted order.
A container that stores unique elements in no particular order, optimized for fast retrieval.
A container adapter providing last-in, first-out access to elements.
A container adapter providing first-in, first-out access to elements.
A container adapter that provides constant time access to the largest element.

Algorithms.details: Sorts the elements in the range [first, last) into ascending order.
Sorts the elements while ensuring that the relative order of equal elements is preserved.
Returns an iterator to the first element in the range that equals the specified value.
Checks if a value exists in a sorted range using binary search.
Returns an iterator to the first element in a sorted range that is not less than the value.
Returns an iterator to the first element in a sorted range that is greater than the value.
Returns an iterator pointing to the element with the largest value in the range.
Returns an iterator pointing to the element with the smallest value in the range.
Reverses the order of the elements in the range [first, last).
Computes the sum of the elements in a range, starting with an initial value.
Returns the number of elements in a range for which a predicate is true.
Algorithms.example: int evens = std::count_if(v.begin(), v.end(), [](int i){ return i % 2 == 0; });

Algorithms.desc: Apply a function to a range
Algorithms.details: Applies a specific function to each element in a range and stores the result in another range.
Algorithms.example: std::transform(v.begin(), v.end(), v.begin(), [](int i){ return i * 2; });

Functions & Lambdas.details: An anonymous function object capable of capturing variables from its scope.
Creates a lambda that captures all used local variables by making copies of them.
Creates a lambda that captures all used local variables by reference.
Creates a lambda that captures only a specific variable by making a copy.
A container that can store, copy, and invoke any callable target (function, lambda, etc.).
Functions & Lambdas.example: std::function<int(int)> func = [](int x){ return x * x; };

Classes & OOP.details: A blueprint for creating objects, where members are private unless specified otherwise.
A blueprint for creating objects, where members are public by default.
An access specifier that allows class members to be accessed from outside the class.

Classes & OOP.desc: Members accessible only within the class
Classes & OOP.details: An access specifier that restricts access to class members to within the class itself.

Classes & OOP.desc: Members accessible in class and derived classes
Classes & OOP.details: An access specifier that allows access within the class and its subclasses.
Allows a specific external class or function to access the private and protected members of this class.
A keyword that represents a pointer to the object for which the member function is being called.

Exceptions.desc: Block of code that may throw
Exceptions.details: Defines a block of code to be tested for errors while it is being executed.
Defines a block of code to be executed if an exception is thrown in the try block.
Signals the occurrence of an error by throwing an object (usually an exception).
Specifies that a function is guaranteed not to throw any exceptions.
